queue.title: No molecule-level culinary data model
queue.description: Tying batch/extraction/potency/terpenes to ingredients, dishes, and courses.

queue.title: No regulator-grade traceability export
queue.description: That proves source material, preparation mapping, delivery, and consumption chain of custody.

queue.title: No deterministic inventory-to-menu execution assistant
queue.description: A first-class answer to "what can I serve now with what remains? " across current stock and guest constraints.

queue.title: Product System (SKUs, Batches, Units)
queue.description: ChefFlow has no concept of products, batches, or units. Maya needs to track 100+ brownies with exact 5mg dosing per unit — but ChefFlow only manages event courses and guests. Without this foundation, she cannot even define what constitutes a "batch" or "unit" in her workflow.

queue.title: Batch Dosing Engine
queue.description: The system lacks any mechanism to calculate THC distribution per unit in a batch. Maya requires precise calculation of total THC input → unit distribution (e.g., 100 brownies at exactly 5mg each), but ChefFlow's recipe system is designed for event courses, not batch production.

queue.title: No market-first menu builder mode
queue.description: Workflow that starts from currently available and priced ingredients, then proposes feasible dish options.

queue.title: No safe-only menu generation mode for high-risk clients
queue.description: Planning still assumes optional review; Rina needs an operational mode where only valid options are visible for a selected client profile.

queue.title: Insufficient hidden-risk and cross-contact signaling at ingredient level
queue.description: Allergen checks exist, but hidden processing and cross-contact risk annotations are not surfaced as explicit hazard tiers in selection flows.

queue.title: No deterministic dose-curve engine

queue.title: No cannabis outcome intelligence loop
queue.description: (cross-event analysis of dose patterns, tolerance, adverse signals, and optimal progression templates).

queue.title: No canonical central dosing engine
queue.description: Jordan’s highest-risk failure mode is inconsistent per-course dose allocation across chefs and events. Cannabis pages and dosage fields exist, but there is no explicitly documented rules engine enforcing dose standards globally.

queue.title: Guest cannabis tolerance history is not modeled as a first-class longitudinal safety signal
queue.description: Repeat guest dosing should improve with historical response and adverse-reaction memory. CRM and event history are strong, but explicit cannabis tolerance evolution and dose-response profile contracts are not documented.

queue.title: Product System (SKUs, Batches, Units)
queue.description: ChefFlow has no concept of products, batches, or units. Maya needs to track 100+ brownies with exact 5mg dosing per unit — but ChefFlow only manages event courses and guests. Without this foundation, she cannot even define what constitutes a "batch" or "unit" in her workflow.

queue.title: Batch Dosing Engine
queue.description: The system lacks any mechanism to calculate THC distribution per unit in a batch. Maya requires precise calculation of total THC input → unit distribution (e.g., 100 brownies at exactly 5mg each), but ChefFlow's recipe system is designed for event courses, not batch production.

queue.title: Sales Channel Tracking
queue.description: \*\* ChefFlow only tracks event sales (dinners), not product sales through pop-ups, drops, or direct channels. Maya's multi-channel sales require movement tracking across channels, but ChefFlow has no product-level sales history.

queue.title: No explicit outcome-based reaction log as first-class feedback
queue.description: Feedback surfaces are broad, but this persona requires structured outcomes such as tolerated, mild reaction, severe reaction, and clinician follow-up.

queue.title: No dedicated safety command center view
queue.description: Dashboard has strong operational cards, but no focused safety board summarizing blocked items, unresolved risks, and per-client verification state.

queue.title: No real-time dosing administration log
queue.description: That records exact delivered mg and immediate guest response in-service.

queue.description: Feedback surfaces are broad, but this persona requires structured outcomes such as tolerated, mild reaction, severe reaction, and clinician follow-up.

queue.title: Ephemeral Event Lifecycle Management
queue.description: ChefFlow lacks a dedicated workflow for one-night events from concept to archive. Kai's model requires rapid setup (24h max) and immediate post-event analysis, but ChefFlow's event system is designed for recurring, long-term operations. Without this, Kai would rebuild everything manually for each drop.

queue.title: Sales Channel Tracking
queue.description: \*\* ChefFlow only tracks event sales (dinners), not product sales through pop-ups, drops, or direct channels. Maya's multi-channel sales require movement tracking across channels, but ChefFlow has no product-level sales history.

queue.title: Drop Engine for High-Demand Releases
queue.description: ChefFlow has no mechanism for handling sell-out flow during high-demand events. When Kai releases an event, the system would require manual intervention to manage 200+ people, causing first-come chaos and message overload.

queue.title: Sales Channel Tracking

queue.title: No hard medical constraint enforcement engine (blocking layer)

queue.title: No explicit offline-first guarantee for mission-critical workflows
queue.description: Reliable local access to inventory, preferences, plans, menus, and task timelines during disconnected work.

queue.title: No documented conflict-safe sync model
queue.description: Safe handling for multi-day disconnected edits and eventual reconnection.

queue.title: V1 strategy conflicts with multi-chef governance need
queue.description: Jordan is a director role; safety and consistency depend on cross-chef standardization and oversight. The blueprint states V1 is single-chef and explicitly out-of-scope for multi-chef platform behavior.

queue.title: Director-level cross-event risk cockpit is incomplete for this persona
queue.description: Jordan needs one glance to see which event/chef/course combinations are dose-risky now. Strong dashboards exist, but no explicit cannabis-risk heatmap or multi-chef exception queue is documented.

queue.title: No deterministic inventory-to-menu execution assistant
queue.description: A first-class answer to "what can I serve now with what remains? " across current stock and guest constraints.

queue.description: Depletion simulation for fixed no-restock windows, storage limits, shelf life, and contingency substitutions.

queue.title: No rapid guest roster churn flow
queue.description: Arrive-today and depart-today actions that update preference relevance in active plans.

queue.title: Audience Curation and Composition
queue.description: While ChefFlow has audience segmentation, it lacks tools to curate energy and cultural fit for Kai's specific audience needs. The system can't filter guests by cultural resonance or event energy levels.

queue.title: Location Adaptation and Venue Constraints
queue.description: ChefFlow doesn't track venue capabilities or adjust setup requirements for each event. Kai's secret locations require rapid reconfiguration, but the system expects fixed venues with permanent capabilities.

queue.title: Compliance evidence is fragmented across multiple screens
queue.description: Legal defensibility depends on one coherent chain from intake → dosing decisions → execution → outcome logs. Compliance tracker + cannabis ledger + events + RSVPs exist, but evidence appears feature-separated rather than a unified attestation timeline.

queue.title: Tiered Access and Waitlist Control
queue.description: The system has no native tiered access permissions or waitlist management. Kai's 200-person waitlists require controlled release waves and priority allocation, but ChefFlow only handles public events or simple booking without access tiers.
